---
title: "The Behavioral Architect: Scaling Health Opportunity through Digital Twins"
subtitle: "Strategic Collaboration Roadmap | Dr. Atheendar Venkataramani"
author: "Yichao Jin, PhD"
date: "2026-04-24"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: false
    code-fold: true
    css: styles.css
---

<style>
.main-container {
    max-width: 1000px;
}
.hero-box {
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, #001f3f 0%, #003366 100%);
    color: white;
    padding: 2.5rem;
    border-radius: 15px;
    margin-bottom: 2rem;
    box-shadow: 0 4px 15px rgba(0,0,0,0.1);
}
.stat-card {
    border-left: 5px solid #003366;
    background: #f8f9fa;
    padding: 1.5rem;
    margin: 1rem 0;
    border-radius: 0 10px 10px 0;
}
.highlight {
    color: #003366;
    font-weight: bold;
}
</style>

<div class="hero-box">
  <h1>Bridging Economic Opportunity & Health Equity</h1>
  <p>Leveraging Behavioral Digital Twins (BDT) to stress-test the impacts of social mobility on population health.</p>
</div>

## 🔬 Research Synergy: The Opportunity Health Lab

Your work on the **Health Effects of Economic Opportunity** establishes the causal framework for how structural shifts shape lives. My framework, the **Behavioral Digital Twin (BDT)**, provides the simulation engine to scale these insights:

*   **Choice under Constraint:** Modeling how marginalized individuals navigate "Low-Opportunity" environments using Discrete Choice Experiments (DCE).
*   **Stress-Testing Policies:** Simulating the health outcomes of upward mobility interventions before large-scale implementation.
*   **Predictive Vulnerability:** Identifying which subgroups are most resilient or susceptible to shifts in the "Economic Safety Net."

---

## 🛠️ The BDT Framework for Opportunity Health

I combine econometric rigor with generative modeling to create individual-level simulations of behavioral response to economic shifts.

::: {.stat-card}
### 1. Generative Population Modeling
*   Creating "Digital Twins" of vulnerable populations using **PATH**, **NHIS**, and lab-based choice data.
*   Modeling the "Architectural" constraints—the latent barriers that prevent healthy choices in low-resource settings.
:::

::: {.stat-card}
### 2. Causal Behavioral Simulation
*   Integrating your findings on economic despair and health into a predictive engine.
*   "What-If" scenarios: Modeling the health impact of guaranteed income, education subsidies, or housing policy shifts.
:::

::: {.stat-card}
### 3. Policy Stress-Testing
*   Providing a technical lever to evaluate the equity-efficiency trade-off in public health interventions.
*   Evaluating "Behavioral Spillover" effects of economic policies onto chronic disease management.
:::

---

## 📈 Collaborative Impact at UPenn

My goal is to align with the **Opportunity Health Lab** to tackle the next generation of social determinants research:

1.  **Economic Opportunity & Chronic Disease:** Using BDT to predict how shifts in local industry impact long-term health behavior (e.g., substance use, nutrition).
2.  **Equity-Centered Intervention Design:** Optimizing policy outreach to ensure that "Opportunity Policies" reach the most behaviorally-vulnerable clusters.
3.  **Structural Racism & Decision Architecture:** Quantifying how systemic barriers warp the decision-making landscape of minority populations.

---

<div style="text-align: center; margin-top: 3rem; padding: 2rem; background: #eee; border-radius: 10px;">
  <h3>"Transforming Economic Insights into Predictive Behavioral Solutions."</h3>
  <p>Contact: Yichao.Jin@UTDallas.edu | [yichao2022.github.io](https://yichao2022.github.io)</p>
</div>
